name: server-project-root-hardcoded
tags: server.py project root worktree static files templates css deploy bug paths.py
body:
hooks/server.py previously had `_PROJECT_ROOT = Path.home() / 'workspace/claude-hooks'` hardcoded. This caused StaticFiles and templates to always serve from the main worktree, so CSS/template changes deployed to claude-hooks-test were invisible — the server silently served stale files from main.

**Fix applied:** All paths now live in `hooks/paths.py`. Import with `from hooks.paths import PROJECT_ROOT as _PROJECT_ROOT`. `PROJECT_ROOT` resolves via `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` — worktree-safe by design.

**Why:** Path.home()-based hardcode doesn't account for the git worktree model (dev/test/main are separate dirs).

**How to apply:** Never hardcode paths in server.py or any module that mounts static files. Always import from `hooks/paths.py`. Verify after deploy with `curl /ui/static/ui.css | grep <changed-rule>`.
